subject: [PATCH 1/4] memcg: store node_id instead of pglist_data pointer

reviews.author: Harry Yoo
reviews.summary: Provided a formal Reviewed-by tag without any specific technical feedback.
reviews.raw_body:
On 5/20/26 2:31 PM, Shakeel Butt wrote:
> The struct obj_stock_pcp stores a pointer to pglist_data for the slab
> stats cached on the cpu. On 64-bit machines, this costs 8 bytes. The
> pointer is not strictly required: NODE_DATA() can recover it from the
> node id. Replace cached_pgdat with int16_t node_id and use NUMA_NO_NODE
> as the "no stats cached" sentinel.
> 
> At the moment all the archs limit MAX_NUMNODES to 1024 so int16_t is
> plenty; a BUILD_BUG_ON() makes sure we notice if that ever changes.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>
> Tested-by: kernel test robot <oliver.sang@intel.com>
> ---

Reviewed-by: Harry Yoo (Oracle) <harry@kernel.org>

-- 
Cheers,
Harry / Hyeonggon

reviews.author: Muchun Song
reviews.summary: Gave a formal Acked-by tag without any specific technical feedback.
reviews.raw_body:
> On May 20, 2026, at 13:31, Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev> wrote:
> 
> The struct obj_stock_pcp stores a pointer to pglist_data for the slab
> stats cached on the cpu. On 64-bit machines, this costs 8 bytes. The
> pointer is not strictly required: NODE_DATA() can recover it from the
> node id. Replace cached_pgdat with int16_t node_id and use NUMA_NO_NODE
> as the "no stats cached" sentinel.
> 
> At the moment all the archs limit MAX_NUMNODES to 1024 so int16_t is
> plenty; a BUILD_BUG_ON() makes sure we notice if that ever changes.
> 
> Signed-off-by: Shakeel Butt <shakeel.butt@linux.dev>
> Tested-by: kernel test robot <oliver.sang@intel.com>

Acked-by: Muchun Song <muchun.song@linux.dev>

Thanks.

2026-05-20.patch_summary: This patch series aims to optimize memory cgroup (memcg) performance by reducing the storage cost of slab statistics on 64-bit machines. The current implementation stores a pointer to pglist_data, which consumes 8 bytes. Instead, it proposes storing the node ID as an int16_t and using NUMA_NO_NODE as a sentinel value for no stats caching. This change is made possible because all architectures currently limit MAX_NUMNODES to 1024, ensuring that int16_t is sufficient.
